title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, Setting New Coding Benchmarks
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4 — the world's best coding model — and Claude Sonnet 4, both featuring extended thinking with tool use, improved memory, and parallel tool execution.
content: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4 on May 22, establishing Opus 4 as the world's best coding model with a 72.5% score on SWE-bench and exceptional performance on long-running agent workflows that can run continuously for several hours. Claude Sonnet 4 achieves a 72.7% SWE-bench score and improves steerability for everyday use cases, with GitHub reporting it will power the new Copilot coding agent. Both models are hybrid reasoning models offering near-instant and extended thinking modes and now support parallel tool use, improved memory capabilities when given local file access, and extended thinking with tool use in beta. Anthropic also made Claude Code generally available, adding VS Code and JetBrains integrations, a GitHub Action for background tasks, and a new SDK for building custom agents. Pricing remains unchanged at $15/$75 per million tokens for Opus 4 and $3/$15 for Sonnet 4.

title: Google Launches AI Mode for All US Users, Signals the Future of Search
summary: At Google I/O 2025, Google rolled out AI Mode — a Gemini-powered conversational search interface — to all US users, integrating deep research, web browsing agents, and personalization into Google Search.
content: At Google I/O 2025, Google announced it is rolling AI Mode — a Gemini-powered chatbot interface — out to all US Google Search users, signaling a fundamental redesign of the world's most-used search engine. AI Mode adds Deep Search for multi-source synthesis, Project Mariner for agentic web browsing, Search Live for camera-based real-time queries, and access to the user's past search history and email for personalization. Google reported that 1.5 billion people are now seeing AI Overviews in search results every month. The company described the traditional results page as 'a construct' being replaced by a flexible, AI-generated canvas that could include dynamically created charts, videos, or web apps tailored to each query. Google executives said Search will look unrecognizable within three years as the focus shifts from organizing information to making it genuinely useful.

title: Google Rolls Out Project Mariner Web-Browsing Agent at Google I/O 2025
summary: Google expanded Project Mariner, its AI agent that autonomously browses and acts on websites, to US subscribers of its new $249.99/month AI Ultra plan, now capable of handling 10 tasks simultaneously.
content: At Google I/O 2025, Google rolled out Project Mariner — its web-browsing AI agent — to US subscribers of the new $249.99-per-month AI Ultra plan, with international support coming soon. The updated Project Mariner now runs on cloud virtual machines, allowing users to keep working while the agent handles tasks in the background and manages up to 10 simultaneous tasks, a major upgrade over the earlier version that required the user's own browser tab. Google is also bringing Project Mariner's capabilities to the Gemini API and Vertex AI for developers. The agent can purchase event tickets, order groceries, and complete other web transactions autonomously, competing directly with OpenAI's Operator and Amazon's Nova Act. Google said it is working with Ticketmaster, StubHub, Resy, and Vagaro to support agentic booking flows, and Project Mariner will soon be accessible inside AI Mode in Google Search.

title: GitHub Introduces Autonomous Copilot Coding Agent at Microsoft Build
summary: GitHub launched an asynchronous Copilot coding agent powered by Anthropic's Claude that independently fixes bugs and adds features, then submits its work as a pull request for developer review.
content: At Microsoft Build 2025, GitHub introduced a Copilot coding agent that autonomously takes on software engineering tasks — fixing bugs, refactoring code, extending tests, and improving documentation — and notifies developers when the work is complete. Powered by Anthropic's Claude 3.7 Sonnet, the agent operates asynchronously, submitting its work as a pull request and appearing to teammates like another developer on the project. GitHub CEO Thomas Dohmke said the agent excels at low-to-medium complexity tasks in well-tested codebases. The agent is available in preview to Copilot Pro+ and Copilot Enterprise users; Copilot now has over 15 million users, four times more than a year ago. GitHub also announced it will open-source the Copilot Chat VS Code extension under the MIT license and is bringing agent mode to JetBrains, Eclipse, and Xcode.

title: OpenAI Launches Codex: A Cloud-Based AI Coding Agent Inside ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI launched Codex, a cloud-based software engineering agent powered by codex-1 (a fine-tuned o3 model) that handles multiple coding tasks in parallel inside ChatGPT.
content: OpenAI launched Codex on May 16, a cloud-based software engineering agent that runs in a sandboxed virtual environment pre-loaded with users' GitHub repositories. Powered by codex-1 — a version of the o3 reasoning model fine-tuned for software engineering using reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks — Codex can write features, fix bugs, answer codebase questions, and run tests, with each task taking between one and 30 minutes. Unlike local coding assistants, Codex can handle multiple tasks simultaneously without blocking the user's computer or browser. OpenAI said the agent is designed to eventually act as a 'virtual teammate' capable of completing work that would take engineers hours or days, and the company already uses it internally to scaffold features and draft documentation. Codex is rolling out to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, and Team subscribers, with Plus and Edu access coming soon, alongside a new codex-mini-latest model optimized for the Codex CLI.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Invents Novel Algorithms, Beats Decades-Old Records
summary: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, an AI system that uses Gemini LLMs and evolutionary search to automatically discover algorithms that outperform the best human-written solutions in math, computer science, and real-world engineering.
content: Google DeepMind introduced AlphaEvolve, a general-purpose AI coding agent that uses the Gemini 2.0 family of models to iteratively generate, score, and evolve algorithms until finding solutions that surpass existing human-written code. In tests across more than 50 classic math and computer science problems, AlphaEvolve matched best-known solutions 75% of the time and found strictly better solutions in 20% of cases — including beating a 56-year-old record for matrix multiplication set by mathematician Volker Strassen in 1969. Google has been running an AlphaEvolve-generated scheduling algorithm across all of its data centers for over a year, recouping 0.7% of the company's total global compute resources. The system also found optimizations that reduced power consumption in Google's TPU chips and sped up Gemini's own training process. AlphaEvolve's evolutionary approach — combining fast Gemini Flash for breadth with Gemini Pro for depth — makes it applicable to any problem whose solutions can be automatically evaluated by a computer.
